```diff
  const nextConfig = {
+   // Enable static export for GitHub Pages
+   output: &#39;export&#39;,
+   
+   // Base path for GitHub Pages (use repository name)
+   // If your repo is named &quot;QuickCart&quot; or &quot;ecommerce-demo&quot;, update accordingly
+   // For root domain, leave basePath empty
+   basePath: process.env.NODE_ENV === &#39;production&#39; ? &#39;/QuickCart&#39; : &#39;&#39;,
+   
+   // Asset prefix for GitHub Pages
+   assetPrefix: process.env.NODE_ENV === &#39;production&#39; ? &#39;/QuickCart&#39; : &#39;&#39;,
+   
+   // Disable image optimization for static export
+   images: {
+     unoptimized: true,
```

---


Creating the GitHub Actions workflow for deployment:


---

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="write">

<details><summary>Tool use: **code_edit** • Edit file: /Users/saikatmaiti/Documents/TechNova/ecommerce-demo/.github/workflows/deploy.yml</summary>

**Chunk 1**
Lines added: 58, lines removed: 1

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,58 @@
- 
+ name: Deploy to GitHub Pages
+ 
+ on:
+   push:
+     branches:
+       - main
+       - master
+   workflow_dispatch:
+ 
+ permissions:
+   contents: read
+   pages: write
+   id-token: write
+ 
+ concurrency:
+   group: &quot;pages&quot;
+   cancel-in-progress: false
+ 
+ jobs:
+   build:
+     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+     steps:
+       - name: Checkout
+         uses: actions/checkout@v4
+ 
+       - name: Setup Node.js
+         uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+         with:
+           node-version: &#39;18&#39;
+           cache: &#39;npm&#39;
+ 
+       - name: Install dependencies
+         run: npm ci
+ 
+       - name: Setup Pages
+         uses: actions/configure-pages@v4
+ 
+       - name: Build with Next.js
+         env:
+           NEXT_PUBLIC_QUANTAROUTE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.NEXT_PUBLIC_QUANTAROUTE_API_KEY }}
+         run: npm run build:gh-pages
+ 
+       - name: Upload artifact
+         uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3
+         with:
+           path: ./out
+ 
+   deploy:
+     environment:
+       name: github-pages
+       url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }}
+     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+     needs: build
+     steps:
+       - name: Deploy to GitHub Pages
+         id: deployment
+         uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4
+ 
```

</details>

</tool-use>

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,216 @@
- 
+ # QuickCart - GitHub Pages Deployment Guide
+ 
+ This guide will help you deploy QuickCart to GitHub Pages.
+ 
+ ## 🚀 Prerequisites
+ 
+ 1. A GitHub account
+ 2. A GitHub repository (named &#96;QuickCart&#96; or any name you prefer)
+ 3. QuantaRoute API key ([Get one here](https://quantaroute.com))
+ 
+ ## 📋 Step-by-Step Deployment
+ 
+ ### 1. Create GitHub Repository
+ 
+ 1. Create a new repository on GitHub
+ 2. Name it &#96;QuickCart&#96; (or update the &#96;basePath&#96; in &#96;next.config.js&#96; if using a different name)
+ 3. Initialize with a README (optional)
+ 
+ ### 2. Push Code to GitHub
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ # Initialize git (if not already done)
+ git init
+ 
+ # Add all files
+ git add .
+ 
+ # Commit
+ git commit -m &quot;Initial commit: QuickCart deployment&quot;
+ 
+ # Add remote (replace YOUR_USERNAME with your GitHub username)
+ git remote add origin https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/QuickCart.git
+ 
+ # Push to main branch
+ git branch -M main
+ git push -u origin main
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### 3. Configure GitHub Pages
+ 
+ 1. Go to your repository on GitHub
+ 2. Navigate to **Settings** → **Pages**
+ 3. Under **Source**, select **GitHub Actions**
+ 4. This will enable automatic deployment via the workflow we&#39;ve set up
+ 
+ ### 4. Add API Key as Secret
+ 
+ 1. Go to your repository on GitHub
+ 2. Navigate to **Settings** → **Secrets and variables** → **Actions**
+ 3. Click **New repository secret**
+ 4. Name: &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_QUANTAROUTE_API_KEY&#96;
+ 5. Value: Your QuantaRoute API key
+ 6. Click **Add secret**
+ 
+ ### 5. Configure Base Path (if needed)
+ 
+ If your repository name is different from &#96;QuickCart&#96;, update the base path:
+ 
+ 1. Open &#96;next.config.js&#96;
+ 2. Update the &#96;basePath&#96; value:
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;javascript
+    basePath: process.env.NODE_ENV === &#39;production&#39; ? &#39;/YOUR_REPO_NAME&#39; : &#39;&#39;,
+    assetPrefix: process.env.NODE_ENV === &#39;production&#39; ? &#39;/YOUR_REPO_NAME&#39; : &#39;&#39;,
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### 6. Trigger Deployment
+ 
+ 1. The GitHub Actions workflow will automatically run when you push to &#96;main&#96; branch
+ 2. You can also manually trigger it: **Actions** → **Deploy to GitHub Pages** → **Run workflow**
+ 3. Wait for the workflow to complete (usually 2-3 minutes)
+ 4. Once completed, your site will be live at:
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;
+    https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/QuickCart/
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ## 🔧 Manual Deployment (Alternative)
+ 
+ If you prefer to deploy manually:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ # Build the project
+ npm run build:gh-pages
+ 
+ # The static files will be in the /out directory
+ # You can deploy this directory to any static hosting service
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Deploy to GitHub Pages Manually
+ 
+ 1. Build the project: &#96;npm run build:gh-pages&#96;
+ 2. Push the &#96;out&#96; directory to the &#96;gh-pages&#96; branch:
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+    git subtree push --prefix out origin gh-pages
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 3. Or use the &#96;gh-pages&#96; package:
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+    npm install --save-dev gh-pages
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;
+    Add to &#96;package.json&#96;:
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+    &quot;scripts&quot;: {
+      &quot;deploy&quot;: &quot;npm run build:gh-pages &amp;&amp; gh-pages -d out&quot;
+    }
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;
+    Then run: &#96;npm run deploy&#96;
+ 
+ ## 🌐 Custom Domain (Optional)
+ 
+ To use a custom domain:
+ 
+ 1. Create a &#96;CNAME&#96; file in the &#96;public&#96; directory with your domain:
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;
+    yourdomain.com
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 2. Update your DNS settings to point to GitHub Pages
+ 3. In GitHub repository settings, add your custom domain
+ 
+ ## 📝 Environment Variables
+ 
+ ### Required for Production
+ 
+ - &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_QUANTAROUTE_API_KEY&#96;: Your QuantaRoute API key
+ 
+ ### Setting in GitHub Actions
+ 
+ The workflow automatically uses the secret you configured:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;yaml
+ env:
+   NEXT_PUBLIC_QUANTAROUTE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.NEXT_PUBLIC_QUANTAROUTE_API_KEY }}
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Local Development
+ 
+ Create a &#96;.env.local&#96; file:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ NEXT_PUBLIC_QUANTAROUTE_API_KEY=your_api_key_here
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Note**: Never commit &#96;.env.local&#96; to git (it&#39;s in &#96;.gitignore&#96;)
+ 
+ ## 🔍 Troubleshooting
+ 
+ ### Build Fails
+ 
+ 1. Check GitHub Actions logs for errors
+ 2. Verify API key is set correctly in secrets
+ 3. Ensure &#96;node_modules&#96; is not committed (check &#96;.gitignore&#96;)
+ 
+ ### 404 Errors on Routes
+ 
+ - Verify &#96;basePath&#96; is correct in &#96;next.config.js&#96;
+ - Check that the repository name matches the basePath
+ - Clear browser cache and try again
+ 
+ ### API Key Not Working
+ 
+ 1. Verify the API key is correct
+ 2. Check that the secret name matches exactly: &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_QUANTAROUTE_API_KEY&#96;
+ 3. Ensure the API key has the required permissions
+ 
+ ### Assets Not Loading
+ 
+ 1. Check that &#96;assetPrefix&#96; matches &#96;basePath&#96; in &#96;next.config.js&#96;
+ 2. Verify images are in the &#96;public&#96; directory
+ 3. Check browser console for 404 errors
+ 
+ ## 📊 Monitoring Deployment
+ 
+ 1. Go to **Actions** tab in your GitHub repository
+ 2. Click on the latest workflow run
+ 3. View build logs and deployment status
+ 4. Check the **Pages** section in Settings for deployment URL
+ 
+ ## 🎯 Post-Deployment
+ 
+ After successful deployment:
+ 
+ 1. Test the application at your GitHub Pages URL
+ 2. Verify location detection works
+ 3. Test the checkout flow
+ 4. Check mobile responsiveness
+ 5. Verify API calls are working
+ 
+ ## 🔄 Updating the Site
+ 
+ To update the site:
+ 
+ 1. Make your changes locally
+ 2. Commit and push to &#96;main&#96; branch:
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+    git add .
+    git commit -m &quot;Your update message&quot;
+    git push origin main
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 3. GitHub Actions will automatically rebuild and redeploy
+ 4. Wait 2-3 minutes for deployment to complete
+ 
+ ## 📚 Additional Resources
+ 
+ - [GitHub Pages Documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/pages)
+ - [Next.js Static Export](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/deploying/static-exports)
+ - [QuantaRoute Documentation](https://quantaroute.com/docs)
+ 
+ ## 🆘 Need Help?
+ 
+ If you encounter issues:
+ 
+ 1. Check the GitHub Actions logs
+ 2. Verify all configuration steps are completed
+ 3. Ensure API key is valid and has proper permissions
+ 4. Check browser console for client-side errors
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ **Happy Deploying! 🚀**
+ 
```

</details>

</tool-use>

```diff
+

+ 
+ ### Quick Deploy Steps

+ 2. Add &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_QUANTAROUTE_API_KEY&#96; as a GitHub secret
+ 3. Enable GitHub Pages with GitHub Actions
+ 4. The workflow will automatically deploy on every push to &#96;main&#96; branch
+
```

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,123 @@
- 
+ # QuickCart 🛒
+ 
+ &gt; A smart e-commerce checkout demo showcasing QuantaRoute Geocoding SDK for efficient address management and location-based services.
+ 
+ ![QuickCart](https://img.shields.io/badge/Next.js-13-black?style=for-the-badge&amp;logo=next.js)
+ ![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-5.2-blue?style=for-the-badge&amp;logo=typescript)
+ ![GitHub Pages](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub%20Pages-Deployed-brightgreen?style=for-the-badge&amp;logo=github)
+ 
+ ## 🎯 Overview
+ 
+ QuickCart is a mobile-first e-commerce demo application that demonstrates the efficiency of using the **QuantaRoute Geocoding SDK** for seamless checkout experiences. The app focuses on an easy, streamlined checkout flow with intelligent location detection and automatic address population.
+ 
+ ## ✨ Key Features
+ 
+ - 🎯 **Smart Location Detection** - Automatic location detection using browser geolocation
+ - 📍 **Auto-Fill Address** - Intelligent address auto-population using QuantaRoute API
+ - 🗺️ **Interactive Maps** - Visual location confirmation with MapLibre
+ - 🛒 **Easy Checkout Flow** - Streamlined 3-step checkout process
+ - 📱 **Mobile-First Design** - Optimized for mobile devices
+ - ⚡ **Fast &amp; Efficient** - Quick address lookup and form population
+ 
+ ## 🚀 Quick Start
+ 
+ ### Prerequisites
+ 
+ - Node.js 18+ installed
+ - QuantaRoute API key ([Get one here](https://quantaroute.com))
+ 
+ ### Installation
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ # Clone the repository
+ git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/QuickCart.git
+ cd QuickCart
+ 
+ # Install dependencies
+ npm install
+ 
+ # Set up environment variables
+ echo &quot;NEXT_PUBLIC_QUANTAROUTE_API_KEY=your_api_key_here&quot; &gt; .env.local
+ 
+ # Run development server
+ npm run dev
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ Visit [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000) to see the app.
+ 
+ ## 📚 Documentation
+ 
+ - **[Full Documentation](./README-QUICKCART.md)** - Complete feature documentation and API details
+ - **[Deployment Guide](./DEPLOYMENT.md)** - Step-by-step GitHub Pages deployment instructions
+ - **[Quick Start Guide](./QUICKSTART.md)** - Quick setup and getting started
+ 
+ ## 🌐 Deploy to GitHub Pages
+ 
+ QuickCart is ready for GitHub Pages deployment out of the box!
+ 
+ 1. Push your code to GitHub
+ 2. Add &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_QUANTAROUTE_API_KEY&#96; as a GitHub secret
+ 3. Enable GitHub Pages with GitHub Actions
+ 4. Your app will be automatically deployed
+ 
+ See [DEPLOYMENT.md](./DEPLOYMENT.md) for detailed instructions.
+ 
+ **Live Demo**: &#96;https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/QuickCart/&#96;
+ 
+ ## 🛠️ Technology Stack
+ 
+ - **Framework**: Next.js 13 (App Router)
+ - **Language**: TypeScript
+ - **Styling**: Tailwind CSS
+ - **UI Components**: shadcn/ui
+ - **Maps**: MapLibre GL
+ - **Geocoding**: QuantaRoute Geocoding SDK
+ 
+ ## 📖 About QuantaRoute
+ 
+ QuantaRoute provides India-specific geocoding services with:
+ - 36,000+ postal boundaries coverage
+ - Sub-100ms response times
+ - Government-level precision
+ - DigiPin technology for unique location codes
+ 
+ Learn more at [quantaroute.com](https://quantaroute.com)
+ 
+ ## 📝 Project Structure
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ QuickCart/
+ ├── app/                    # Next.js app directory
+ │   ├── checkout/          # Checkout page
+ │   ├── setup/             # Setup guide
+ │   └── page.tsx           # Home page
+ ├── components/            # React components
+ │   ├── address-form.tsx   # Smart address form
+ │   ├── location-detector.tsx
+ │   └── mini-map.tsx       # Map component
+ ├── contexts/              # React contexts
+ │   └── cart-context.tsx   # Cart state management
+ ├── lib/                   # Utilities and types
+ └── .github/               # GitHub Actions workflow
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ## 🤝 Contributing
+ 
+ This is a demo application. Feel free to fork and customize for your needs!
+ 
+ ## 📄 License
+ 
+ This is a demo application showcasing QuantaRoute Geocoding API integration.
+ 
+ ## 🙏 Acknowledgments
+ 
+ - [QuantaRoute](https://quantaroute.com) for providing the geocoding API
+ - [Next.js](https://nextjs.org) for the amazing framework
+ - [shadcn/ui](https://ui.shadcn.com) for beautiful UI components
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ **Built with ❤️ to showcase QuantaRoute&#39;s powerful geocoding capabilities**
+ 
+ For questions or support, please refer to the [documentation](./README-QUICKCART.md) or [deployment guide](./DEPLOYMENT.md).
+ 
```

   - Added Next.js, Node.js, and editor-specific entries
   - Added OS-specific files and build artifacts

2. Configured Next.js for static export
   - Enabled `output: 'export'` for static site generation
   - Set basePath/assetPrefix for GitHub Pages (configurable via environment variable)
   - Disabled image optimization (required for static export)

3. Updated address form for client-side API calls
   - Removed dependency on Next.js API routes
   - Modified `address-form.tsx` to call QuantaRoute API directly from the client
   - Uses `NEXT_PUBLIC_QUANTAROUTE_API_KEY` environment variable

4. Removed API routes
   - Deleted `/app/api/location-lookup/route.ts`
   - Deleted `/app/api/digipin-reverse/route.ts`
   - Prevents build errors with static export

5. Updated package.json
   - Added `build:gh-pages` script for production builds
   - Added deployment scripts

6. Created GitHub Actions workflow
   - Automatic deployment on push to `main` branch
   - Builds and deploys to GitHub Pages
   - Uses repository name automatically for basePath
   - Configures environment variables including API key from secrets

7. Created documentation
   - Created `DEPLOYMENT.md` with step-by-step deployment instructions
   - Updated `README-QUICKCART.md` with deployment section
   - Created main `README.md` as project entry point

### Next steps

1. Push to GitHub:
   ```bash
   git add .
   git commit -m "Configure for GitHub Pages deployment"
   git push origin main
   ```

2. Configure GitHub repository:
   - Go to Settings → Pages → Source: select "GitHub Actions"
   - Go to Settings → Secrets → Actions → add `NEXT_PUBLIC_QUANTAROUTE_API_KEY`

3. Deploy:
   - The GitHub Actions workflow will run automatically
   - The app will be live at: `https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/QuickCart/`

### Important notes

- Repository name: If your repository is not named "QuickCart", update the `basePath` in `next.config.js` or it will auto-detect from the repository name
- API key: Must be set as a GitHub secret named `NEXT_PUBLIC_QUANTAROUTE_API_KEY`
- Static export: The app is now a static site, so no server-side features (API routes, server components with dynamic data, etc.)
- Client-side API calls: All QuantaRoute API calls are made directly from the browser

Ready for GitHub Pages deployment. See `DEPLOYMENT.md` for detailed instructions.
